acceptance_checks: Worksheet records the 401 unauthenticated API result instead of pretending active listings were captured.
Worksheet separates listing, data-access, local-baseline, and submission gates.
At least fifteen fields are defined for future candidate rows.
At least four candidate classes are defined.
At least eight hard kill reasons are defined.
No Kaggle login, token, data download, rules acceptance, notebook run, submission, public comment, or payment action occurred.

candidate_classes.promotion_gate: specific competition rules and eligibility reviewed
candidate_classes.route: Kaggle featured category sorted by prize

candidate_classes.first_local_action: screen for data ethics, external data rules, and compute scale
candidate_classes.promotion_gate: data provenance and publication/IP terms reviewed
candidate_classes.route: Kaggle research category sorted by prize

candidate_classes.promotion_gate: do not count as money path unless cash prize exists

candidate_classes.first_local_action: read kernel/submission constraints and design local harness
candidate_classes.promotion_gate: no Kaggle notebook run or submit without account/compute approval
candidate_classes.route: competition docs/files indicate notebook or code submission

cli_refresh_plan.command_shape: Human/browser read of https://www.kaggle.com/competitions sorted by prizes/deadlines
kaggle competitions list --group general --category featured --sort-by prize -v
Use public/open fixtures or synthetic fixture locally; no Kaggle download or submission

recommended_next_local_proof: Create a no-login Kaggle competition row fixture from manually saved public listing/rules snippets, then run the worksheet scorer and prove all rows either have a next local baseline action or a kill reason. Keep Kaggle account, API token, rules acceptance, data download, notebook execution, submission, payment, public action, worker/runtime, and model/MCP calls blocked.

scoring_rubric.good: at least 14 days to first useful local baseline
data can be inspected after approved rules review; no private/medical ambiguity
simple local baseline or public tutorial can be reproduced without paid compute
low/moderate team count or niche expertise advantage
external data, team, code, and IP rules are unambiguous
tabular/NLP/vision/agentic task matches available local skills
eligibility and tax/payment route can be reviewed before submission

observations: Official competition listing exists but is JavaScript-heavy in text fetches.
Use it as a human/browser review source only after an approved read-only browser request.
Read-only unauthenticated probe returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized.
Automated current listing refresh requires an approved Kaggle credential/API-token route.
The official Kaggle CLI can list competitions, download competition data, and submit to competitions.
These capabilities must be split into separate scout, data-access, and submission gates.
Kaggle CLI authentication requires a Kaggle account and token or OAuth flow.
Any credential generation, account login, or API-token storage is gated.
The CLI supports competition list filters by group, category, prize sort, deadlines, teams, and created date.
It also supports files, download, submit, submissions, leaderboard, and discussion topics commands.
Kaggle competitions can be paid or unpaid.
Submission, leaderboard, and prize/IP conditions vary by competition and must be read in the specific rules.
Recent Kaggle ecosystem research frames the platform as a broad competition, notebook, model, dataset, and discussion ecosystem.
Use discussions and prior writeups as learning signals, not as permission to submit.
Prior competition lane work kept account, official data, submission, paid compute, and public action blocked.
Reuse that gate posture for Kaggle prize scouting.

summary: Kaggle is a valid money-competition lane, but current automated active-listing capture is gated by Kaggle authentication and competition-specific rules. This packet creates the scout worksheet, source route plan, scoring rubric, kill reasons, and exact gates for a later approved listing refresh.
